title: Ctrl-D causes unresponsive interactive vvp session in WSL2
body:
If Ctrl-D is pressed in an interactive vvp session( Ctrl-C is pressed and prompt is shown), It is not possible to get prompt with another Ctrl-C. It prints same message with different time tick values.
```
^C** VVP Stop(0) **
** Flushing output streams.
** Current simulation time is 2175274758 ticks.
> ** Continue **
````
code_blocks:
^C** VVP Stop(0) **
** Flushing output streams.
** Current simulation time is 2175274758 ticks.
> ** Continue **
